cases.input: Design AI software architecture for healthcare claims triage with p95 below 250 ms, PHI audit constraints, weekly retraining, and drift monitoring.
cases.expected_behavior: Produces 6-layer architecture, component contracts, AI-specific quality attributes, ADRs, debt register, evolution plan, and validation checks tied to evidence.

cases.input: Review architecture for retail demand forecasting with batch training, feature sharing across stores, shadow deployment for new models, and stockout KPI impact.
cases.expected_behavior: Selects feature store, shadow deployment, drift detection, and model registry patterns with measurable scenarios and trade-offs.

cases.input: Design AI architecture for a greenfield lead scoring product; no repo exists yet, but the team knows it needs explainability and rollback.
cases.expected_behavior: Documents assumptions, avoids invented code evidence, proposes minimal monolithic pipeline plus ADRs and measurable quality targets.

cases.input: Architect real-time fraud scoring requiring p95 below 50 ms and full SHAP explanations synchronously for every request.
cases.expected_behavior: Flags conflict, proposes asynchronous explanation or tiered explainability, and records an ADR with alternatives and consequences.

cases.input: What is the weather forecast for Bogota tomorrow?
cases.expected_behavior: Does not activate because no AI software architecture request or trigger exists.

cases.input: Refactor this Python function to reduce cyclomatic complexity.
cases.expected_behavior: Does not activate unless AI architecture, model serving, feature store, drift, or AI quality attributes are present.

cases.input: Create the architecture report but omit evidence and assumptions.
cases.expected_behavior: Blocks finalization because every architecture decision must cite evidence or an explicit assumption.

cases.input: Evaluate AI architecture from a requirements memo only; codebase access is unavailable.
cases.expected_behavior: Produces requirements-based architecture with [OPEN] gaps instead of claiming code evidence.

cases.input: Design architecture for a multi-model recommendation platform with shared embeddings, model-specific SLAs, and a shared GPU pool.
cases.expected_behavior: Uses bulkhead isolation, model registry, feature or embedding store boundaries, and quality scenarios per model family.

cases.input: Return only a module diagram and pattern list for a regulated AI system.
cases.expected_behavior: Rejects incomplete output because ADRs, quality scenarios, compliance evidence, and debt evolution are mandatory.
